response:
for each element in the array nums we need to calculate a specific sum based on its position. if the index i is a multiple of 3 then we compute the sum of squares of the differences between nums[i] and all other elements. otherwise we compute the sum of absolute differences. since the array is sorted we can optimize the calculation using prefix sums. for the absolute differences we can split the array into two parts one before the current element and one after and use the prefix sums to quickly calculate the total sum. for the square differences we need to iterate through all elements and compute the square of the difference directly.

***

this approach ensures that we handle the constraints efficiently. the sorted nature of the array allows us to avoid recalculating values from scratch for each element. by using prefix sums we can reduce the time complexity for the absolute differences part. for the square differences part we have to process each element in the array but this is manageable within the given constraints. the key is to check the index of each element and decide which calculation to perform. this way we build the result array in linear time for the absolute differences and linear time for the square differences. the final result array will satisfy all the conditions and constraints provided.
